item.title: I’m back illustrating too! [OC]
item.description:
I’m back illustrating as well as making my comics!
For cat tax I offer you, Ms. Bean in all her kitty glory! If you want to support my comics do check out my Patreon! https://www.patreon.com/AmyRevives
item.dc:creator: AmyRevives

item.title: Bit chilly innit?? [OC]
item.description:
Every darn day! I can’t wait for summer :,D good thing I love snuggling in warm jumpers and thick fluffy blankets!
Of course the mighty Chonker cat tax! If you want to support my comics do check out my Patreon! https://www.patreon.com/AmyRevives
item.dc:creator: AmyRevives

item.title: Reality in Ukraine: While recording a message to the residents of Kherson, the head of the City Military Administration, Roman Mrochko, came under enemy fire. He just stood up and continued his address.
item.dc:creator: vladrichdemaclant

item.title: Arizona Iced Tea has kept its iconic 99-cent price for 32 years
item.description: Meanwhile, Supermarket chain Meijers, waited months till their special needs teenage employee racked up over $100 in unpaid lunch so they could press charges. Also, they wrote him up when he waited in line to pay https://imgur.com/gallery/supermarket-chain-meijers-waited-months-till-special-needs-teenage-employee-racked-up-over-100-unpaid-so-they-could-press-charges-also-they-wrote-him-up-when-he-waited-line-to-pay-rZDzk1p

item.title: What Happened Today #76
item.description: They’re dismantling the system from every angle, and this week, they escalated. The IRS is being weaponized, science boards are purged, disaster programs axed, and even Medicare data is being turned into a surveillance tool. But resistance is biting back: a judge threatens contempt over illegal deportations, Schumer blocks Trump’s DOJ picks, and California sues over economic sabotage. While the headlines scream, they’re quietly bleeding public service: cutting AmeriCorps, slashing species protections, and killing off foreign disinfo teams. Don’t just watch the fire. Go fight the arson. Speak out, show up, and stop the sabotage. Subscribe free at https://govbrief.today #GovBriefToday #Resist 1. https://go.govbrief.today/boasberg-contempt 2. https://go.govbrief.today/vanhollen-salvador 3. https://go.govbrief.today/bondi-abregogarcia 4. https://go.govbrief.today/doge-americorps 5. https://go.govbrief.today/irs-directfile 6. https://go.govbrief.today/labor-doge 7. https://go.govbrief.today/ezkZdoge-verainst 8. https://go.govbrief.today/dod-library-books 9. https://go.govbrief.today/california-tariffs 10. https://go.govbrief.today/hhs-budget 11. https://go.govbrief.today/ny-wind 12. https://go.govbrief.today/pentagon-cirlce-widens 13. https://go.govbrief.today/harvard-taxexempt 14. https://go.govbrief.today/dhs-harvard 15. https://go.govbrief.today/medicare-immigration 16. https://go.govbrief.today/enangered-species 17. https://go.govbrief.today/women-minorities-science 18. https://go.govbrief.today/disinformation-office 19. https://go.govbrief.today/schumer-block
item.dc:creator: GeorgeBounacos

item.title: Are you over the age of 40 and don't know where to begin with new Metal, or maybe you just want to escape the algorithm? Well, here's some new flavors of black coffee to get you started.
item.description:
We'll start off with an easy one. Nightwish. If you're into straight up symphonic metal. They've been around for quite some time, more than 20 years now, but they pioneered the Symphonic Metal genre, and IMO, are still the best at it, with their current singer. Deep Silent Complete, Ever Dream, Bye Bye Beautiful, Amaranthe, Elan and Alpenglow will give you a pretty good taste of each style the band has had throughout their lead singer changes. Oceanborn, and Endless Forms most beautiful are their two best albums.
Another easy one. Ghost has been exploding in popularity for almost a decade now. If you're into 80s metal with a heavy occult flavor. Ghost has been described as KISS meets ABBA, and that is not an inaccurate description. Their older material is a lot darker with less of the pop flavor. Recommendations are Cirice, Square Hammer, Griftwood, and Dance Macabre. Dance Macabre is easily their best track, and Impera or Meliora is their best album.
Now to some more unknown stuff. Orden Ogan is my personal all-time favorite band, due to them not having, in my opinion, one single bad track, and a discography that is long and consistently amazing to listen to. Orden Ogan is for those of you who want straight up heavy metal with great melody and amazing riffs. This is also a band that produces the best guitar solos in the game. I recommend FEVER, Heart of the Android, December, or Gunman to start. FEVER was my introduction to them, and 'December' is my favorite track. Best album is their latest "Final Days."
Amorphis is another band that I would put in my top 5 of all time. Like Orden Ogan they don't have a single bad track with their current current lead singer. If you're into melodic death metal, IMO, there are no better death growls, and clean vocals than are produced by this band. The Wanderer, Amongst Stars, The Bee, House of Sleep, and Silent Waters are where you should start. I'm not being dramatic when I say I can't name a favorite by this band. Every song is a banger, and their albums are consistently 8-9/10s. I rank them and Orden Ogan as my favorite bands so far.
Ascendia is a Canadian band that I discovered because one of their music videos came up as an ad on youtube. Ascendia is if you're into.... Well, actually, I don't know how to compare this band. Power Metal perhaps? With a unique flavor. The vocalist has a very distinct voice and vocal style. It's borderline operatic, and for a male vocalist that's highly unusual. Their song "Remember Me" is the only youtube ad I've never skipped in my life. Remember Me, The Lion and the Jester, At the End of it All, and Faded Away are you starting points, though that's half of their only album at the moment.
Dethklok if you're into straight up death metal, and don't mind somewhat goofy lyrics (if you're even listening to them at all). Despite being a metal "band" from a comedy cartoon, Brendon Smalls is, IMO, a GODLIKE musician who writes, and performs all of the music in the show, (except drums). Go into the Water, Go forth and Die, Awaken, and Thunderhorse are your starters here. You can pick any album, as Dethalbum's I - IV are basically identical (in a good way).
Dance with the dead if you like synthwave or electronic at all, but wished there were more rocking guitar riffs and solos. Almost entirely instrumental, DwtD is very niche and probably won't be for everyone. Their cover of "I Got Five On It" (instrumental or vocal version with Brandon Saller of Atreyu), their cover of the Nightmare on Elm Street theme, That House, and Adrift are good places to start, with my personal favorites being Surrender and Stoic. Best albums are either B Sides or The Shape.
Do you want to just have your face pounded by riffs, and growls? Meshugguh is where you wanna be. Veterans of the genre, and not technically "new", these guys are here if you just want crushing riffs, and death growls, but also never want to know what time signature a song is in. Seriously, I don't think it CAN get "heavier" than these guys. My personal tastes tend to be less extreme than what Meshugguh has on offer, but Break Those Bones Whose Sinews Gave It Motion is, IMO the only place you need to start with these guys, but Demiurge, Bleed, and Koloss are great tracks, with Koloss and ObZen being their stand out albums IMO.
Mick Gordon. I don't know how "old school" you all might be, but if you haven't heard, Mick Gordon is the composer of the Doom(2016) and Doom Eternal videogame soundtracks that are described as "A metal album with a free video game attached". He also composed the Soundtrack for the first two seasons of Killer Instinct(2013) which, IMO is his best work so far, for the reason being that it highlights his talent as a composer, and not just as a rocksmith. Check out Rip and Tear, The only thing they fear is You, Hinnamatoom, and Type-03, with Typo-03 having one of my favorite breakdowns in music.
Lastly I'll leave you with a personal favorite, The Vision Bleak. They have some of THE best guitar riffs in their music, and it's all very horror themed, with Elizabeth Dane, The Wood Hag, The Demon of the Mire, and He Who Paints the Black of Night as must-listen tracks. The Witching Hour, and Set Sail to Mystery are their best albums, but Into the Unknown, their latest has a really interesting feel that I keep coming back to.
item.dc:creator: JaXm

item.title: The 1955 Mercedes-Benz 300 SLR "Uhlenhaut" Coupé recently sold at auction for $143 million, making it the most expensive car in the world.
item.description: From Wikipedia : In spite of its name and strong resemblance to both the streamlined 1952 W194 Le Mans racer, and the iconic 1954 300SL Gullwing road car it spawned, 1955 300 SLR was not derived from either. Instead, it was based on the wildly successful 2.5-litre straight-eight-powered 1954–1955 Mercedes-Benz W196 Formula One champion, with the engine, enlarged to 3.0 litres for the sports car racing circuit and designated "SL-R" for Sport Leicht-Rennen (eng: Sport Light-Racing). All were the work of Daimler-Benz's design chief Rudolf Uhlenhaut. The 300 SLR was front mid-engined, with its long longitudinally-mounted engine placed just behind the front axle to better balance front/rear weight distribution. A brazed steel tube spaceframe chassis carried ultra-light Elektron magnesium-alloy bodywork (having a relative density of 1.8, less than a quarter of iron's 7.8), which contributed substantially to the low vehicle mass of 901 kg (1,986 lb) for the roadster,[10] 1,117 kg (2,463 lb) in the case of the coupé. The M 196 S engine is a naturally aspirated straight-eight engine with a bore and stroke of 78.0 mm (3 in), resulting in a displacement of 2,982 cc (182 cu in). The engine has a peak power output of 310 PS (228 kW) at 7400 rpm, with maximum sustained power of 276 PS (203 kW) at 7000 rpm.[11] Its maximum torque is 31.7 kp⋅m (311 N⋅m) at 5950 rpm, giving the engine a very high BMEP of 1.31 MPa (190 lbf/in2). Like in the W 196 R, the engine was canted to the right at 53 degrees to lower the car's profile, resulting in slicker aerodynamics and a distinctive bulge on the passenger side of the hood shared with the streamlined Type Monza Formula one car. To reduce crankshaft torsion, power takeoff was from the centre of the engine via a gear rather than at the end of the crankshaft. Daimler-Benz fitted the engine with a single desmodromic intake valve, and a single desmodromic exhaust valve per cylinder, actuated by two spur-gear driven overhead camshafts. The fuel system is a direct fuel injection system with a mechanically driven eight-plunger inline injection pump made by Bosch. https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Mercedes-Benz_300_SLR
item.dc:creator: TerribleBot
